titular: Decline in Oil Prices Despite Truce in Lebanon War, but Strait of Hormuz Remains a Concern
cuerpo:
The price of oil has decreased despite the onset of the global pandemic due to COVID-19, resulting from a recent truce in the Lebanon war. However, the Strait of Hormuz remains a zone of significant concern for international petroleum interests.

Expected drops in supply due to the war and diplomatic negotiations have caused market imbalances, leading to a sudden drop in prices. Yet, Iran's blockade of traffic in the Strait of Hormuz continues to pose an important risk to global petroleum security.

The Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) has announced that it is working closely with the governments of producing and consuming countries in the region, to ensure continuous oil flow in case of traffic disruption.
resumen_seo: El precio del petróleo ha caído por más que desde la pandemia de COVID-19 debido a un acuerdo de alto el fuego en la guerra de Líbano. Aun así, el bloqueo de Irán en el Estrecho de Hormuz sigue siendo una zona de preocupación para los intereses petroleros internacionales.
